report.executive_summary: This contract contains 4 high-risk clause(s) and 0 medium-risk clause(s) that require attention before signing. Key issues include: Clause provides no measurable service levels, uptime guarant, Vendor's liability cap of $500 is far below market standard , 5-day dispute window is unreasonably short; creates risk of , 90-day notice period for non-renewal is significantly longer, GDPR Art. 28 mandates a DPA for any processing of EU.

missing_clauses.heading: Data Processing Agreement (DPA)
missing_clauses.reason: GDPR Art. 28 mandates a DPA for any processing of EU personal data by a processor.

clause_table.heading: 1. SERVICES
clause_table.reason: Clause provides no measurable service levels, uptime guarantees, or remedies. "Commercially reasonable efforts" is too vague and unenforceable. Market standard requires specific uptime percentages (e.g., 99.9%) with service credits for failures.
clause_table.original_text:
1. SERVICES
Vendor agrees to provide Client with access to the Acme SaaS Platform
("Platform") on the terms set out in this Agreement. Vendor will use
commercially reasonable efforts to maintain availability of the Platform.
clause_table.redline:
1. SERVICES
Vendor shall provide Client with access to the Acme SaaS Platform ("Platform") on the terms set out in this Agreement. The Platform shall achieve an uptime of at least 99.9% each calendar month, measured as the percentage of total minutes in the month during which the Platform is operational and accessible to Client, excluding scheduled maintenance with prior notice and force majeure events. If the monthly uptime falls below 99.9%, Vendor shall credit Client's account an

("Platform") on the terms set out in this Agreement.

Vendor will use
commercially reasonable efforts to maintain availability of the Platform.

The Platform shall achieve an uptime of at least 99.9% each calendar month, measured as the percentage of total minutes in the month during which the Platform is operational and accessible to Client, excluding scheduled maintenance with prior notice and force majeure events. If the monthly uptime falls below 99.9%, Vendor shall credit Client's account an

playbook_check.standard: 99.9% monthly uptime; service credits of 10% per 1% downtime below SLA
playbook_check.acceptable_range: 99.5% minimum uptime with any credit mechanism

clause_table.heading: 2. FEES AND PAYMENT
clause_table.reason: Could not assess — defaulting to Low risk.
clause_table.original_text:
2. FEES AND PAYMENT
2.1 Client shall pay Vendor a monthly subscription fee of $5,000 per month.
2.2 Invoices are due and payable within 7 days of the invoice date. Late
payments shall accrue interest at 5% per month.
2.3 All fees are non-refundable.

playbook_check.standard: Vendor retains all pre-existing IP; only custom work-for-hire transfers

clause_table.heading: 3. TERM
clause_table.reason: Could not assess — defaulting to Low risk.
clause_table.original_text:
3. TERM
3.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue for an
initial term of 12 months.

clause_table.heading: 4. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
clause_table.reason: Vendor's liability cap of $500 is far below market standard ($50k min or 12-mo fees). Effectively eliminates Client's remedy. No exceptions for fundamntal breaches.
clause_table.original_text:
4. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
4.1 In no event shall either party be liable for indirect, incidental, or
consequential damages.
4.2 Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, Vendor's total aggregate
liability to Client for any claims arising under or related to this
Agreement shall not exceed $500 (five hundred US dollars).
clause_table.redline_reason: Redline generation failed: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'strip'

clause_table.heading: 5. CONFIDENTIALITY
clause_table.reason: Could not assess — defaulting to Low risk.
clause_table.original_text:
5. CONFIDENTIALITY
5.1 Each party agrees to keep confidential all non-public information of
the other party disclosed in connection with this Agreement.
5.2 This confidentiality obligation shall survive for a period of 3 years
following termination of this Agreement.
5.3 Confidential information shall not include information that is publicly
available or independently developed.

playbook_check.standard: 30-day notice for convenience; 30-day cure period for breach

clause_table.heading: 6. PAYMENT DISPUTES
clause_table.reason: 5-day dispute window is unreasonably short; creates risk of waiving legitimate billing disputes. Market standard is typically 30+ days.
clause_table.original_text:
6. PAYMENT DISPUTES
6.1 Client must raise any payment dispute in writing within 5 days of
receiving an invoice. Disputes not raised within this period are deemed
waived.
clause_table.redline:
6. PAYMENT DISPUTES
6.1 Client must submit any payment dispute in writing within 30 days of receiving an invoice. Disputes not raised within this period are waived.

diff.text: any payment dispute in writing within
receiving an invoice. Disputes not raised within this period are

clause_table.redline_reason: The change extends the dispute window from 5 to 30 days to align with common market practice and ensure clients have sufficient time to identify and raise legitimate billing issues without risk of automatic waiver.

clause_table.heading: 7. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
clause_table.reason: Could not assess — defaulting to Low risk.
clause_table.original_text:
7. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
7.1 Client acknowledges and agrees that all intellectual property rights in
and to the Platform, including all modifications, enhancements, and
derivative works, and all other work product created by Vendor in
connection with this Agreement, shall be and remain the sole and exclusive
property of Client. Vendor hereby assigns to Client all right, title, and
interest in and to such intellectual property, including all pre-existing
work, tools, and methodologies used by Vendor.

playbook_check.standard: Vendor retains all pre-existing IP; only custom work-for-hire transfers

clause_table.heading: 8. GOVERNING LAW
clause_table.reason: Standard Delaware governing law and exclusive venue; acceptable but may be inconvenient for remote clients.
clause_table.original_text:
8. GOVERNING LAW
8.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with
the laws of the State of Delaware. Any disputes shall be resolved
exclusively in the state or federal courts located in Delaware.

clause_table.heading: 9. TERMINATION
clause_table.reason: Standard 30-day termination for convenience; immediate for material breach. Balanced mutual terms align with market standards.
clause_table.original_text:
9. TERMINATION
9.1 Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon 30 days
written notice.
9.2 Either party may terminate immediately upon written notice if the other
party materially breaches this Agreement.

playbook_check.standard: 30-day notice for convenience; 30-day cure period for breach

clause_table.heading: 10. GENERAL
clause_table.reason: Standard entire agreement and no-oral-modification clauses; typical boilerplate with minimal risk.
clause_table.original_text:
10. GENERAL
10.1 This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties
with respect to its subject matter.
10.2 No amendment to this Agreement shall be effective unless made in
writing and signed by both parties.

clause_table.heading: 11. RENEWAL
clause_table.reason: 90-day notice period for non-renewal is significantly longer than standard 30 days, creating high risk of unintentional binding renewal for client.
clause_table.original_text:
11. RENEWAL
11.1 Upon expiration of the initial term, this Agreement will automatically
renew for successive 12-month periods.
11.3 Either party may prevent automatic renewal by providing written notice
to the other party at least 90 days prior to the end of the then-current
term. Failure to provide such notice within this window shall result in
automatic binding renewal.
clause_table.redline:
11. RENEWAL
11.1 Upon expiration of the initial term, this Agreement will automatically renew for successive 12-month periods.
11.3 Either party may prevent automatic renewal by providing written notice to the other party at least 30 days before the end of the current term. If notice is not provided within this period, the Agreement will automatically renew for an additional term.

11.3 Either party may prevent automatic renewal by providing written notice
to the other party at least

Failure to provide such notice within this window shall result in
automatic binding renewal
If notice is not provided within this period, the Agreement will automatically renew for an additional term

clause_table.redline_reason: The notice period for non-renewal is reduced from 90 days to 30 days to align with market standards and lower the risk of unintended binding renewals for the client.

playbook_check.standard: 30-day written notice to cancel before renewal

clause_table.heading: MISSING: Data Processing Agreement (DPA)
clause_table.reason: GDPR Art. 28 mandates a DPA for any processing of EU personal data by a processor.
clause_table.redline_reason: GDPR Art. 28 mandates a DPA for any processing of EU personal data by a processor.

playbook_check.standard: A Data Processing Agreement must be in place before any EU personal data is processed.

action_checklist.action: Add SLA with 99.9% uptime commitment and service credits.
Redline liability cap to 12-month fees or $50,000 minimum before signing.
